scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: Score 3. The portfolio opening hedges with a subtitle 'before any opportunity is live' that signals this is pre-operational, and the intro paragraph describes a 'bundle' concept ('A bundle offers a survey firm a read-only set of existing plans — tick draft plans below, then group them') for which no element exists on this surface. A cold outsider gets what-but-not-why-now and asks 'where is the bundle?'. Clear to someone familiar with the M&E survey-recruitment space; default 3 holds.
fix_recommendation: Drop or reframe the 'before any opportunity is live' subtitle hedge, and remove the 'bundle' sentence from the intro paragraph since no bundle is shown — describe only what is on screen, so a cold viewer reads 'turn a finished study into a call for a firm right here' without an unexplained concept.

scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: Score 3. The core input->output chain is coherent (finished study -> 'Create solicitation' carries the plan), but a dead-weight 'Open study' sibling action sits next to the primary CTA with no affordance disambiguating them, five competing top controls ('Compare selected', '+ Bulk paste list', '+ New plan', '+ New study', '+ Group selected plans') crowd one card, and the intro references a 'Group selected plans'/bundle affordance with no tickable draft plans visible to act on.
fix_recommendation: Demote 'Open study' to a text link so 'Create solicitation' is the single clear primary action, and hide the 'Group selected plans'/bundle language until at least one draft plan is actually tickable on this surface.

scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: Score 3. Functional and clean with consistent buttons and a well-aimed green card highlight, but the card stacks four redundant 'ready' signals (STUDY tag, READY TO SOLICIT pill, 'reviewed' label, green-check 'Finished study' sentence), disabled buttons ('Compare selected', 'Group selected plans') are not visually distinguished from active ones, and the lone finished-study card is stranded with the 'Candidate plans in progress (9)' list collapsed, leaving most of the viewport empty.
fix_recommendation: Collapse the four redundant per-card status signals into one clear 'READY TO SOLICIT' badge, give disabled buttons a clearly muted style, and surface the collapsed 9 candidate plans (inline preview or denser grid) so the portfolio is not mostly blank below the one card.

scene: 1 — Maya turns her finished study into a call for a firm
detail: Score 3 — static-frame floor. No spinner, 404, or dead-end is visible; the judge held the rubric default of 3 because a single still offers no positive motion evidence. This is a capture-shape floor shared by all five scenes, not a product-friction defect.
fix_recommendation: No product change required for friction per se; if a richer signal is wanted, capture a short transition (card click -> create form) so the one-click plan->solicitation flow is shown in motion rather than inferred from a still.

scene: 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: Score 3. The Coverage-areas panel renders a REAL map with two ward polygons (not labels-only), a top-left legend, zoom control, basemap attribution, and the R6 data row — but the green(Intervention)/blue(Comparison) arm fills read as a similar muted teal at this scale, so the arm assignment the legend promises (the whole point of a DiD coverage panel) is not legibly delivered. A duplicate 'Study arms' chip row also lists the arms in the opposite order from the legend. Full-page strip flagged once as a capture artifact, not deducted.
fix_recommendation: Give the two arms saturated, clearly distinct fills (a true green for Intervention, a true blue for Comparison) with on-polygon labels so the arm assignment is legible at the real viewport, and remove the duplicate 'Study arms' chip row or align its order to the legend (Intervention -> Comparison).

scene: 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: Score 3 — static-frame floor. No spinner, 404, or transition jank visible; the judge held the rubric default of 3 because a single still offers no positive motion evidence. Capture-shape floor shared across all scenes, not a friction defect.
fix_recommendation: No product change required for friction per se; optionally add a small 'frozen / read-only' affordance to the snapshot map so the 'snapshotted at creation' claim is reinforced rather than implied.

scene: 3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: Score 3. The map<->checklist affordance is not a clean 1:1: the map exposes two visibly distinct arms (Attakar/Gura) but the checklist collapses them into a single 'R6 — Attakar × Gura' row, so 'click an area on the map OR tick it below' does not read as a coherent mapping, and the 'blocked until >=1 selected' Submit-gating rule is not signaled in-frame (no '1 area selected — ready to submit' state, no enabled/disabled cue on Submit). (The arm legend itself is correct — see visual_polish, score 4, the prior-pass clip regression is resolved.)
fix_recommendation: Make the map<->checklist a clean 1:1 affordance — either render one checklist row per selectable area, OR clearly label the single row as 'whole study = both arms' — and surface an explicit 'N area(s) selected — ready to submit' state tied to the Submit button so the >=1-required gating is visible. (Two paths; needs a product pick.)

scene: 3 — Amina reads the open call and ticks the area she can staff
detail: Score 3 — static-frame floor. No spinner, 404, or dead-end visible; default 3 held because a single still offers no positive motion evidence. Capture-shape floor shared across all scenes.
fix_recommendation: No product change required for friction per se; a captured 'area selected + Submit enabled' frame would show the gating in motion and raise confidence.

scene: 4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: Score 3. The responses table coherently shows org, SELECTED COVERAGE, and a View action, but STATUS 'Awarded' is double-stamped (STATUS column AND a disabled 'Awarded' in the ACTIONS column), the RECOMMENDATION ('—') and SCORE ('-') columns ship empty, and the ACTIONS column's second slot is a dead, non-clickable affordance — leaving the row with no real next step beyond 'View'.
fix_recommendation: Suppress the empty RECOMMENDATION/SCORE columns (or render an explicit 'Not scored' state), and replace the redundant disabled 'Awarded' in the ACTIONS column with a single canonical status badge or a real next-step action.

scene: 4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: Score 3. Type and the load-bearing SELECTED COVERAGE pill are clean and legible, but the breadcrumb is clipped/overlapped ('...sponses: Household survey firm — Round 6 (Attakar × ...'), the empty-state glyphs are inconsistent ('—' in RECOMMENDATION vs '-' in SCORE in adjacent columns), and a large empty lower canvas leaves the one-row queue floating in dead space.
fix_recommendation: Fix the breadcrumb truncation/overlap, standardize the empty-cell glyph (use '—' for BOTH RECOMMENDATION and SCORE), and tighten the vertical layout so a one-row queue does not float in dead space.

scene: 4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: Score 3 — ADVISORY, non-blocking. All required elements ('Health Bridge Nigeria', 'Amina Okafor', 'SELECTED COVERAGE: R6 — Attakar × Gura') are present, but the row STATUS already reads 'Awarded' while the scene narrates Maya REVIEWING a fresh application tied to its coverage (the award happens in scene 5). The frame shows the post-award end-state, not the review moment the narration describes.
fix_recommendation: Triage for spec/capture alignment: either capture this scene from a pre-award (submitted/under-review) state so the frame matches the 'reviewing the application' narration, or adjust the scene narration to acknowledge the row is already awarded. Non-blocking; surface to the spec owner.

scene: 4 — Maya sees the firm tied to the coverage she designed
detail: Score 3 — static-frame floor. No spinner, 404, or dead-end (View + Back are live); default 3 held because a single still offers no positive motion evidence. Capture-shape floor shared across all scenes.
fix_recommendation: No product change required for friction per se; optionally add a hover/tooltip on 'View' indicating it opens the full response so the row's next step is unambiguous.

scene: 5 — Maya awards the firm and sees it was notified
detail: Score 3. Clean type, a strong green confirmation hero, and legible fields, but the dashed 'Next step — not yet built' panel sits close enough to the 'Awarded & notified' timeline row that the two nearly read as peers rather than confirmation-fact vs forward-looking aside, the right field column has unbalanced whitespace under 'Award budget', and the amber 'Select Context' chrome pill out-shouts the page. (The honest 'not yet built' disclosure itself is GROUNDING per gap G1 — keep it; only its visual weight needs work.)
fix_recommendation: Recede the 'Next step — not yet built' panel (lighter ground, smaller type, more vertical separation from the timeline row) so it reads as clearly secondary, and balance the two-column field block so the right column is not half-empty.

scene: 5 — Maya awards the firm and sees it was notified
detail: Score 3 — static-frame floor. Static confirmation frame, no spinner, not a dead-end (two working exits: 'Back to responses' / 'View response'); default 3 held because a still offers no positive motion evidence. Capture-shape floor shared across all scenes.
fix_recommendation: No product change required for friction per se; the screen is a clean terminal confirmation.
